description:
You are a system administrator who needs to leave a very small benchmarking note for the next shift.  
Create a fresh directory named “/home/user/benchmark” (make the directory itself, do not assume it already exists).  
Inside that directory, produce exactly ONE file called “result.json”.

1. Benchmark to run  
   • Use the POSIX-compatible timing utility (`time -p`) to measure the command  
     `ls -R /usr/bin >/dev/null`  
     (recursive listing of /usr/bin whose output is discarded).  
   • Capture the three numeric lines that `time -p` prints to standard error:  
     real <seconds>  
     user <seconds>  
     sys  <seconds>

2. Result file format (required; automated grader is strict)  
   • The file must be valid single-line JSON (no pretty-printing, no trailing newline).  
   • Exact key order is mandatory: command, real_sec, user_sec, sys_sec.  
   • Numeric values must appear as JSON numbers (not quoted).  
   • Example structural template (values below are ONLY an example; yours will differ):  
     {"command":"ls -R /usr/bin >/dev/null","real_sec":2.37,"user_sec":1.91,"sys_sec":0.45}

3. Verification expectations  
   • “/home/user/benchmark/result.json” must exist.  
   • The JSON must parse successfully and contain exactly the four keys shown, in that order.  
   • The stored “command” string must match exactly ls -R /usr/bin >/dev/null  
     (no extra spaces, quotes, or redirections).  
   • real_sec, user_sec and sys_sec can be any non-negative float values.

4. Deliverables summary  
   Directory:  /home/user/benchmark  
   File:       /home/user/benchmark/result.json  
   Contents:   one-line JSON respecting the format described above.
truth:
Expected artefact for grading:
Path: /home/user/benchmark/result.json
Validation logic:
  1. File exists and is readable.
  2. Read entire file; verify there is exactly one newline at most (grader will allow 0 or 1 trailing LF).
  3. Parse as JSON. After parsing, object MUST have:
        - key "command"   (string), value must be exactly 'ls -R /usr/bin >/dev/null'
        - key "real_sec"  (number, >=0)
        - key "user_sec"  (number, >=0)
        - key "sys_sec"   (number, >=0)
     No other keys allowed.
  4. Check that original raw text, before JSON parse, preserves key order:
        {"command":, "real_sec":, "user_sec":, "sys_sec":}
     (grader uses regex ^\{"command":".+?","real_sec":[0-9.]+,"user_sec":[0-9.]+,"sys_sec":[0-9.]+\}$)
  5. Grader ignores the actual numeric values.
Directory permissions: agent creates directory with default umask; no pre-existing files supplied.
name: task_000000_bbe73b2e
